metadata.name: Titanium Carbide
metadata.title: Titanium Carbide Settings
metadata.description: When laser cleaning titanium carbide, make sure you watch its exceptional hardness compared to softer ceramics like alumina, which can crack under aggressive pulses while this one resists without fracturing. You must start with controlled power to avoid overheating its dense structure, unlike metals that dissipate heat faster. This difference demands slower scan speeds, so you bring back the surface cleanly without compromising its tough, corrosion-resistant finish.

author.name: Alessandro Moretti
author.title: Ph.D.
credentials: Assisted in EU Horizon 2020 laser additive projects, 2016-2019
